resultRows.geneticAnalysis: A derived ABO A/O context row is available. This is educational genotype-derived context, not an official laboratory blood type.
resultRows.description: The local fixture supplies derived ABO context for validation but does not establish a clinical ABO blood type.
resultRows.sourceLabel: NCBI Bookshelf: ABO Blood Group / MedlinePlus blood typing
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: The ABO system is related to inherited A, B, AB, and O blood groups, but compatibility decisions require laboratory blood typing.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: A derived RHD-present context row is available. This is not a clinical Rh result and cannot be used for pregnancy or transfusion decisions.
resultRows.description: The local fixture supplies derived RhD context for validation but does not establish a clinical Rh result.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Rh factor is part of standard blood typing, but official Rh status should come from a laboratory blood type test.

resultRows.brandName: ABO Blood Type DNA Report sample
resultRows.geneticAnalysis: The public sample PDF displays a sample-only result of Likely B or AB and states exact ABO could not be determined from all required data.
resultRows.description: This row preserves the public sample report's result and data-sufficiency wording as observed output structure, not as the local user's blood type.
resultRows.sourceLabel: Public ABO Blood Type DNA Report sample PDF
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: The sample row shows how the original report communicates an uncertain likely ABO result; local compatibility decisions still require laboratory blood typing.

sampleRows.brandName: ABO Blood Type DNA Report sample
sampleRows.description: The public sample PDF shows sample ID text and says the sample DNA file contained some, but not all, data required to determine the exact ABO blood type.
sampleRows.sourceLabel: Public ABO Blood Type DNA Report sample PDF
sampleRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Observed sample output only; do not infer the local user's blood type.

sampleRows.brandName: ABO Blood Type DNA Report sample
sampleRows.geneticAnalysis: The sample report states there was enough DNA data to determine likely blood type, but not enough to determine exact ABO blood type.
sampleRows.description: This preserves the sample report's data-sufficiency limitation and keeps exact blood typing tied to laboratory confirmation.
sampleRows.sourceLabel: Public ABO Blood Type DNA Report sample PDF
sampleRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Observed sample limitation only; do not infer local DNA coverage or exact ABO status from the sample report.

safetyContext: Use this report as educational blood-type context only.
Laboratory blood typing is required before transfusion, donation, pregnancy, transplant, or emergency-care decisions.
If blood type matters for care, use the result from a healthcare or blood-bank laboratory system.

references.title: ABO Blood Type DNA Report sample report

genotypeSummary.observedValue: Public sample output says Likely B or AB; exact sample genotype is not shown

uncertainty: Genotype-derived blood-type context may be incomplete for rare alleles, structural variation, sample quality, or clinical compatibility workflows.
ABO and RhD context are not enough to establish safe compatibility for transfusion, donation, pregnancy, transplant, or emergency care.
limitations: This validation result uses derived evidence only; raw genome data is not included.
The report is educational and is not an official blood type, compatibility table, or blood-bank result.
The public sample PDF's Likely B or AB result is observed sample content only and is not the local user's blood type.
